description_long: This is a dark-colored, twin-engine military airplane with a distinctive twin-boom tail and a central fuselage. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, with visible propellers on each engine nacelle. The fuselage features a glazed nose section, likely for a bombardier or navigator, and a cockpit positioned just behind it. The tail section is characterized by an H-tail configuration, with two vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer. Markings on the wings and tail suggest a World War II-era American aircraft, with white star insignias and serial numbers.
description: A dark, twin-engine military airplane with a twin-boom H-tail, mid-mounted straight wings, and American insignia.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the twin-boom tail, mid-mounted straight wings, and American star insignia on the wings and tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the twin propellers, glazed nose, and the wide wingspan with visible engine nacelles.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view emphasizing the undercarriage, twin-boom tail, and the symmetrical layout of the wings and stabilizers.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the H-tail configuration, twin vertical stabilizers, and the alignment of the engines and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible dihedral or anhedral angle, no winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines mounted on the wings with visible propellers.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main gear units, appears retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit located just behind the glazed nose, likely multi-seat with a framed canopy.
